pi_items.source: task.json description, export_result.sh line 7, setup_task.sh line 11

Calculated from design spec: Base 15,000 mm³ - pilot hole ~2,281 mm³ - mounting holes ~170 mm³ - countersinks ~51 mm³ - fillets ~100-150 mm³ = ~12,200-12,400 mm³. Range allows for implementation variations (filleting depth, countersink angles). Consistent with M3 mounting holes and 31mm NEMA 17 standard spacing verified from authoritative sources.

Calculated from design spec. Range 11,000-14,000 accounts for variations in fillet implementation, countersink depth, and manufacturing tolerances. Consistent with specified 50x50x6mm base plate and NEMA 17 M3 mounting hole standard.

task.json description: '50mm x 50mm, 6mm thick'. Verified against NEMA 17 faceplate standard of 42mm x 42mm (motor faceplate), with 50mm x 50mm mounting plate providing adequate overhang for M3 mounting.

task.json description line 4: 'Create 4 mounting holes using the Hole tool (do NOT use Pocket for these)'. Verified in verifier.py line 67-69, which explicitly checks for 'PartDesign::Hole' in feature_types.

Web search: Multiple authoritative sources confirm NEMA 17 standard mounting hole spacing is 31mm center-to-center in square pattern. Task description line 4 specifies exactly this. Sources: motormicro.com, engineersedge.com, RepRap wiki.

Web search: Multiple sources confirm NEMA 17 uses M3 (3mm diameter) mounting holes. Task description specifies 'Metric M3'. Sources: motormicro.com, servocity.com, adafruit.com, amazon.com product listings.

pi_items.metadata_value: Not in metadata, but context note
pi_items.source: Web search: NEMA 17 faceplate dimensions are standardized at 1.7 x 1.7 inches = 42 x 42mm. Task plate is 50x50mm. Sources: motormicro.com, engineersedge.com, mosaic-industries.com.

privileged_info_summary: The NEMA 17 stepper motor mounting plate design is based on real industry standards. The plate dimensions are 50mm x 50mm x 6mm, which exceeds the standard NEMA 17 motor faceplate size of 42mm x 42mm. The four mounting holes use M3 metric screws with 31mm center-to-center spacing in a square pattern, both industry-standard specifications for NEMA 17 motors. The volume is calculated to fall between 12,200-12,400 mm³ for the complete design (with base volume 15,000 mm³ reduced by pilot hole, mounting holes, countersinks, and fillets), falling well within the specified range of 11,000-14,000 mm³. The task explicitly requires use of FreeCAD's PartDesign::Hole tool for the mounting holes, as verified by the verifier script.
